name: Caponata of Eggplant
recipeIngredient: 1 large Spanish onion, chopped into 1/2-inch dice
2 medium eggplant, cut into 1/2-inch cubes (yield: 4 cups)
2 teaspoons fresh thyme leaves or 1/2 teaspoon dried thyme
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large sauté pan, sauté onions, garlic, pine nuts, currants, and chili flakes in olive oil for 4 to 5 minutes over medium-high heat until softened.
2. Add eggplant, sugar, cinnamon, and cocoa powder; continue cooking for an additional 5 minutes.
3. Stir in thyme (fresh or dried) and tomato sauce; add balsamic vinegar, salt, and pepper.
4. Bring the mixture to a boil, then lower the heat and simmer for 5 more minutes.
5. Allow the dish to cool to room temperature before serving on crostini or in the middle of the table with crostini on the side for guests to serve themselves.
Yield: 8 servings
